message:
feat(fnp-python): re-export 19 numpy class types (br-franken_numpy-k74v.3)

Expose numpy class types (broadcast, busdaycalendar, dtype, errstate,
finfo, flatiter, iinfo, int_, long, matrix, memmap, ndarray, ndenumerate,
ndindex, nditer, poly1d, recarray, record, ufunc) as fnp_python
attributes via numpy passthrough. Disjoint from the dtype-scalar set
re-exported in daebe80: `uint` and `ulong` live there, not here.

Passthrough strategy rather than pyclass wrapping: the k74v.3 bead
explicitly allows re-export for parity-oracle mode. A future
architectural decision can re-back specific names (ndarray, dtype, ufunc)
with native fnp-python pyclass types without disturbing user-visible
behavior — `fnp_python.<name> is numpy.<name>` today, and can become
`fnp_python.<name> is FnpNdArray` later.

Platform / version notes:
- int_, long: aliases that changed between NumPy 1.x and 2.x; treated as
  version-optional (tested bidirectionally — if numpy has it, fnp_python
  must match identity; if numpy doesn't, fnp_python must also not).
- memmap: numpy exposes it as a class backed by ndarray + file; fnp-io
  has native support that could eventually replace this passthrough.

Test: module_reexports_numpy_class_types_matching_numpy asserts identity
(`ours.is(&theirs)`) for every universal_required name (17 names) and
platform-symmetric handling for int_ / long. Verified via rch exec --
cargo test -p fnp-python --lib module_: 5 passed, 0 failed (including
the 3 earlier module-parity tests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
